general:
This page allows you to enter the information for creating your publication.

On this page, you can:

  • Click on next to the item you want to enter or edit a piece of information,
  • Type into the input box and then click the icon to save,
  • Click the Preview button above to see how this page will look to the public with your changes so far, or
  • After entering all of your publication information, click on the Submit for Review button above to submit your draft for review and publication. A pop-up window will help you manage the submission.

title.label: Title
title.general:
The title should be written in plain language and include sufficient detail to facilitate search and discovery.

If this publication can be considered a supplement to a literature publication, consider using the same title with an extra annotation, like ': Supplementary Data'.
title.edit: Type the title into the input box and then click the icon to save.

authors.label: Authors
authors.general:
This section allows you to create and edit a list of authors or collaborators who contributed to the production of this resource. (Consider including those whose contribution falls under one of the 14 roles listed in the Credit Contributor Roles Taxonomy.)

To be consider a Data Publication (rather than just a Public Data Resource, the publication must include an author list. Just like a literature publication, you are urged to include ORCIDs and affiliations for each author.
authors.list: Click to add a new author. Click to update a previously added author, or click to remove one from the list. If you've made changes to an author, you undo those changes by clicking . Clicking will close the author list for editing.
authors.add: Fill in the values in the open author form. You can quickly add authors from NIST by typing their name into NIST People Lookup and selecting a matched name in the drop-down list. When done, click to save the author to the list, or click to discard the edits you've made.
authors.edit: Edit the values in the open author form. When done, click to save the author to the list, or click to discard the edits you've made.
authors.dragdrop: To change the order of authors listed, click on on the left of each name, drag it to the right place in the list, and release the mouse button to drop it in place.

contactPoint.label: Contact Point
contactPoint.general: The contact point is a person or group at NIST that can respond to questions about this resource.  Every publication must have an up-to-date contact point at NIST.  In particular, the email address must be a NIST address, but it can be a group email list.
contactPoint.add: To add a single person as the contact, type the person's name into the NIST People Lookup and select their name from the drop-down list. To set a group as the contact, enter the group's name or title in the Name box and its email address into Email. (Lookup of group email addresses is not currently supported.) Click the icon to save the contact info.
contactPoint.edit: To set the contact to a single person, type the person's name into the NIST People Lookup and select their name from the drop-down list. To change the contact to a group, enter the group's name or title in the Name box and its email address into 'Email'. (Lookup of group email addresses is not currently supported.) Click the icon to save the new contact info.

landingPage.label: Visit Home Page
landingPage.general:
The Home Page is the user's entry point for this publication. It provides contextual information and access to the resource itself (e.g., download links for data). Importantly, the DOI (digital object identifier) will point to this URL.

If you wish for this NIST Public Data Repository page to be your home page, leave this box blank and click to close the entry field. If you would like to point to another home page (which you maintain), enter the URL here.
landingPage.add:
Enter a working URL for a home page other than this repository landing page. Click to save changes.

[Link to an FAQ: Can I have my DOI point to a different page that the one in the PDR?]
landingPage.edit:
Enter a working URL for a home page other than this repository landing page. Click to save changes.

See also FAQ: Do I have to use a DOI (Digital Object Identifier)? Can I use my own homepage?

doi.label: Identifier
doi.general: Normally, the identifier shown here is a digital object identifier (DOI).  Use this identifier to reference this publication in other publications.  For more information on what a DOI is and how to use it, see [Link to an RAQ: What's a DOI and why do I care?].

version.label: Version
version.general:
You can update this publication after it's been published for the first time with both minor edits (like tweaking the abstract) or major changes, like adding or updating files. We track the changes by assigning a version to the publication. Readers will have access to past versions by opening up the version history displayed here.

Version labels follow a semantic pattern; for more information on the meaning of each digit, see the [Link to FAQ: What does the version label refer to and how is it assigned?]

description.label: Description
description.general:
Describe your resource here in a few human-readable sentences. Consider this the abstract of this publication. If there is an associated paper for this publication, it's okay to paste in its abstract, but you should edit it to focus on the resource presented in this publication.

An ideal abstract is one or two paragraphs long, and should include:

  • Why this resource was produced,
  • A summary of what is included in this publication (and perhaps how it is organized), and
  • What conclusions were realized from the resource or how the resource is useful.
It may also include tips for how to make the best use of this resource (but many details should be reserved for separate README files).
description.edit: An empty line in the description (i.e. hitting the enter key twice) will be treated as a paragraph break. After adding or editing the text, click to save.

topic.label: Research Topics
topic.general: You are in Research Areas section where you can select topics from the NIST Research Taxonomy.  If you have marked this publication to be part of a collection, you can add collection-specific topics as well.  Select one or more topics that the resource addresses.  All publications must select at least one topic for the NIST R&D list.
topic.add: You can browse the available topic areas in the clickable list shown; click on to open up sub-topics. Alternatively, you can type a word in the filter box to find all terms that contain that word. Select a term to add by double-clicking it in the list. Added topics will appear under Selected Topics. To remove a topic area, click on to the right of its name. Click to save your topics.
topic.edit: You can browse the available topic areas in the clickable list shown; click on to open up sub-topics. Alternatively, you can type a word in the filter box to find all terms that contain that word. Select a term to add by double-clicking it in the list. Added topics will appear under Selected Topics. To remove a topic area, click on to the right of its name. Click to save your updates.

keyword.label: Keywords
keyword.general: Add subject keywords that help both technical and non-technical users discover this resource.  Consider adding words or phrases that a reader might put into a search query, particularly those that are not found in the description above.  We recommend entering three to five keywords.
keyword.add: Enter the keyword phrase into text box. Hit Enter (or a comma) to end the keyword phrase and add it to the list. After all keywords have been added, click to save. Click on the next to an added keyword to remove it.
keyword.edit: Enter the keyword phrase into text box. Hit Enter (or a comma) to end the keyword phrase and add it to the list. After all keywords have been added, click to save. Click on the next to an added keyword to remove it.

components.label: External Links
components.general:
In this section, can add links to artifacts that should be considered part of this publication but which are not stored directly in the NIST Public Data Repository. This can include:
  • a public software respository (e.g. in GitHub),
  • externally hosted documentation
  • an external web site where one can download data
  • a specialized web tool (e.g. an on-line database) where one can find or calculate data
  • an approved site that mirrors copies of data you are uploading as part of this publication
Do not include links to published articles; these should be added to the References section below. All links added here must exist and be publicly accessible at submission time. Do not include links to sites restricted to the NIST-internal intranet (e.g. inet pages, NIST-GitLab repositories).
components.list: Click to enter a URL to an external site with information related to this resource. To edit an previously added link, click by it label.
components.add: Enter a URL for the external site into Link URL. A brief title for the link (used as the link's text label) is recommended. You can provide a description of what's available at the link via About this link. Click to save the link. To not save the link, click .
components.edit: Update the selected URL as needed. A brief title for the link (used as the link's text label) is recommended. You can provide a description of what's available at the link via About this link. Click to save the link. To abort the update, click .
components.dragdrop: To change the order of URLs listed, click on on the left of each link, drag it to the right place in the list, and release the mouse button to drop it in place.

references.label: References
references.general: You are in the References section where you can add references or citations related to this resource. You can reference both traditional literature articles or other data or software publications. If this publication is considered a digital supplement to another article, you should include that article here.
references.list: To add a reference, click . Click to make changes to an existing reference. Click to remove a reference from the list.
references.add:
The easiest way to add a reference that currently exists is by providing its DOI: Click on the DOI entry method and then enter it into the DOI box; then click the checkbox to retrieve the reference citation text from the publisher.

Alternatively, you can enter all of the reference information by hand by choosing the Reference Data entry method. Check In Preparation if the reference has not been published, yet. The Citation Text entry method allows you to enter the exact text as it should appear on the page.

If the reference is an article for which this digital publication is a supplement to, click Yes to the Reference Type question. This article will be highlighted at the top of the page.

Click to add the reference to your references list.
references.edit:
You have opened up an existing reference in your references list; edit the details as needed. If a DOI was not included when you first added this reference but does now, enter the DOI URL in the DOI box; click the checkbox at its right to update the citation details to those provided by its publisher.

If the reference is an article for which this digital publication is a supplement to, click Yes to the Reference Type question. This article will be highlighted at the top of the page.

The text in the citation box is what will actually appear on the page. This text is normally automatically generated; however, you can customize the citation text by clicking on the lock icon next to it.

Click to save the reference, or click to abort any changes to the reference.
references.dragdrop: To change the order of references listed, click on on the left of each reference, drag it to the right place in the list, and release the mouse button to drop it in place.

Revision.label: Revision
Revision.general:
Placeholder for Revision:

Metadata Update: Placeholder for description.
File removal: Placeholder for description.
Data change (minor): Placeholder for description.
Data change (major): Placeholder for description.
New file addition: Placeholder for description.

isPartOf.label: Collection Membership
isPartOf.general: If this publication should be part of one of our special-purpose domain collections, you can request for inclusion here.  Note that if you do, the collection maintainer will be asked to approve that your publication should be added.
isPartOf.edit:
Click on one of the named collections to request that this publication be added to it. Then click to save.

[Link to an FAQ: Should or can my publication be added to a collection?]

completed:
This publication looks good! We have no more suggestions.

If you are satisfied with the content, click on 'Submit for Review' to proceed with review and publication.
